strengths: The persona has a concrete whole-portfolio problem rather than generic interest in investing.
Local Hong Kong details affect trust, language use, discovery paths, and data-sharing comfort in functional ways.
The concept reaction is likely to generate useful tension around institutional trust, RM involvement, and premium analytics pricing.
weaknesses: Her finance awareness is relatively high, so she may not represent lower-engagement retail customers.
Some banking behaviors are inferred from affluent Hong Kong norms rather than a named exact bank stack.
Because she is articulate and structured, she may appear slightly more orderly than many real users in the same segment.
uncertainties: Her exact threshold for paying may vary depending on how her primary bank bundles the feature with existing relationship tiers.
Her willingness to aggregate MPF and insurance data could shift if the user experience is exceptionally strong or weak.
Her reaction to BlackRock-style institutional branding could move more positive or more conflict-sensitive depending on how prominently the brand is displayed.
human_review_priorities: Check whether the bilingual explanation needs even stronger Cantonese-specific realism for spoken interview use.
Pressure-test whether her investable assets, income, and product complexity feel balanced for affluent retail rather than edging toward private banking.
Review whether the sensitivity around data sharing and RM visibility is calibrated correctly for Hong Kong multi-bank customers.
